meaning: Error Correction Code,纠错码;Error Checking and Correction,错误检查与纠正
examples: In the winter, ECC can be heated by passing an electric current through it, thereby preventing ice buildup.
Construction costs for ECC are much higher than for standard concrete, so your proposal is probably not justifiable on economic grounds.
Much x86 hardware has poor, limited, or non-existent support for ECC memory.
Currently Enterprise systems ship with Error Correcting Code (ECC) memory, but most consumer systems do not.
But on Monday the ECC once again altered its formula for counting the contested votes.
